title: Astronauts Return Home from Space Adventure
summary: The Artemis II astronauts have come back to Earth after a big space trip. They landed in the ocean near California. These astronauts went farther into space than anyone before. They are Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Jeremy Hansen. In space, their bodies change because there is no gravity. Their muscles and bones can get weaker. But astronauts are trained to handle this. They say being in space is amazing and they would go again. Now, doctors will check them to make sure they are healthy. They will then go to a space center in Houston. Many people have been to space, but this crew's trip was short compared to others.

questions.question: How does this crew's trip compare to others?
options: It was the same as all others

background_read: Space is a place far above Earth where there is no air or gravity. Astronauts are special people who train for years to go to space. They ride in rockets and live in spaceships. In space, things float because there is no gravity to pull them down. This can make astronauts' bodies change, so they exercise to stay strong. When they return to Earth, doctors help them get used to gravity again. The Artemis II mission is part of NASA's plan to explore space more.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Artemis II astronauts: Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Jeremy Hansen.
WHAT: They returned to Earth after a space trip, landing in the ocean, and will be checked by doctors before going to Houston.
WHERE: They landed off the coast of California and will go to Johnson Space Center in Houston.
WHY: To share their experience and recover from the effects of space travel, as their bodies change without gravity.

perspectives.description: Astronauts find space travel rewarding despite challenges like weak muscles.
